step_by_step_reasoning: The whiteboard depicts Spryker's Application Composition Platform (ACP), designed to connect Packaged Business Components (PBCs / SaaS) with Tenant PaaS applications symmetrically. The left-to-right flow starts with a message from a PBC passing through API Gateway, triggering a Lambda that retrieves routing rules from DynamoDB, and forwards the message to an SNS FIFO topic, which routes it sequentially into a Tenant SQS FIFO queue for consumption. The right-to-left flow operates identically but in reverse, enabling bi-directional, decoupled, and ordered communication.

graph.name: Spryker: Decoupled SaaS and PaaS Application Integration Platform
graph.notes: Decoupled bi-directional FIFO messaging platform connecting external SaaS components with tenant applications securely using token-based isolation.

nodes.name: PBC (Packaged Business Component)
nodes.notes: External SaaS partner application acting as message producer or consumer.

nodes.name: API Gateway (PBC)
nodes.notes: Ingests API calls and messages from the PBC side.

nodes.name: Routing Lambda (PBC)
nodes.notes: Applies authorization and dynamic routing rules from DynamoDB.

nodes.name: DynamoDB Tenant Register
nodes.notes: Central registry storing routing rules and tenant details.

nodes.name: SQS FIFO (Tenant)
nodes.notes: Buffers ordered messages for consumption by the tenant application.

nodes.name: Tenant App
nodes.notes: External tenant PaaS application processing asynchronous messages.

nodes.name: API Gateway (Tenant)
nodes.notes: Ingests API requests and events from the Tenant side.

nodes.name: Routing Lambda (Tenant)
nodes.notes: Applies tenant-side routing rules fetched from DynamoDB.

nodes.name: SQS FIFO (PBC)
nodes.notes: Buffers ordered messages for consumption by the PBC SaaS platform.
